mnemonics: EULER'S PATH: Even Vertices Lead to Easy Routes (An Eulerian circuit exists if all vertices have even degree; an Eulerian path exists if exactly zero or two vertices have odd degree.)
keyPoints: An Eulerian circuit is a closed trail that visits every edge exactly once.
A connected graph is Eulerian if and only if every vertex has an even degree.
An Eulerian trail (not necessarily closed) exists if and only if exactly zero or two vertices have an odd degree.
commonMistakes: Confusing Eulerian paths/circuits (which cover every edge once) with Hamiltonian paths/circuits (which cover every vertex once).
Assuming that all vertices must have an even degree for any Eulerian path to exist, forgetting the case where exactly two vertices have an odd degree for an open trail.
